title: Text must meet WCAG AA contrast ratios
summary: AI-generated interfaces frequently use low-contrast text that fails WCAG AA minimum contrast requirements, creating readability barriers.
instruction: Ensure all text meets WCAG AA contrast: 4.5:1 for normal text and 3:1 for large text (18px+ bold or 24px+ regular). Verify with a color contrast tool before choosing text and background combinations.
rationale: Low-contrast text is the most common accessibility failure. Users with visual impairments, outdoor lighting, or low-quality displays are disproportionately affected. WCAG AA is the legal minimum in many jurisdictions including the EU and US federal procurement.

violations: Body text at 16px using #999 on white background (~2.8:1, fails 4.5:1).
Placeholder text (#CCC) on white (~1.6:1) used as an input label.
controls: Body text at 16px using #4A4A4A on white (~8.6:1, passes AA).
Large heading text using #666 on white (~5.4:1, passes AA for large text).
borderline: Inverted text on image overlays where background luminance varies - requires text-shadow fallback or dynamic contrast.

exceptions.description: Decorative text not intended to be read (background watermarks, design patterns), disabled controls where reduced contrast communicates state, and logos in brand color schemes.

detector.signalDescription: Parse CSS color and background-color pairs, compute relative luminance per WCAG formula, calculate contrast ratio. Flag pairs below 4.5:1 (normal text) or 3:1 (large text).
limitations: Dynamic colors from CSS custom properties require runtime computation.
Background images with text overlays cannot be statically analyzed.
Multi-layer backgrounds (gradient over image) require rendering.

scenarios: Text on gradient backgrounds where contrast varies - may pass at some points and fail at others.
Disabled elements where reduced contrast is intentionally communicative.
mitigations: Exclude disabled and decorative elements. For gradient backgrounds, check contrast at multiple sample points.

fixturePlan.description: A page with body text at #999 on white background and light gray placeholder text.
Same page with body text at #333 on white background passing WCAG AA.
A card with text over a gradient where contrast passes on dark end but fails on light end.
